sanskrit:
सर्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम्।
कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम्॥
transliteration:
sarva-bhūtāni kaunteya prakṛtiṃ yānti māmikām |
kalpa-kṣaye punas tāni kalpādau visṛjāmy aham ||
literal_meaning: All beings, O son-of-Kuntī, enter into My nature at the dissolution of an aeon; at the start of an aeon I send them forth again.
contextual_meaning: The psyche experiences periodic total withdrawal (dissolution) and re-projection (creation) of its inner cosmos; nothing is ever lost, only recycled through the Self’s ground.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic stillness; the mind glimpses that every personal catastrophe is a micro-dissolution within a larger, safe container.
trigger_scenario: Losing your job, identity, or relationship and realizing life will re-configure—your roles dissolve but your core Self remains intact.
anchor_text: Every ending is the Self pressing reset; every beginning, the same Self pressing play.
